scenarios.name: fresh clone, no recent sentinels, no in-cycle upstream movement (bootstrap)
scenarios.setup: fresh clone of bare origin; integrity/ empty of sync-workspace-runs-*.json sentinels; config fallback_baseline_at = 2026-05-30T00:00:00Z; origin/main commit ts = 2026-05-31T08:38:40Z (newer than fallback)
scenarios.expected_action: pull-moved-auto-sync (bootstrap case: no prior sentinel + upstream newer than fallback baseline triggers initial sync)
scenarios.interpretation: On true cold start with no sentinel history, the bootstrap fallback triggers one initial sync against the upstream. After this sentinel lands, subsequent runs compare against it (the real on-disk source of truth), not the fallback. This matches the design in DIRECTIVE-20260531-004 Q1 answer.

scenarios.name: external agent commits to origin while work-init has older sentinel
scenarios.setup: second clone pushes a commit to origin; work-init still at older SHA; sentinel ts=2026-05-31T08:30Z exists, origin HEAD ts=2026-05-31T08:39:11Z (newer)
scenarios.interpretation: The PRIMARY production path. PROP-064 was supposed to catch this case via PRE_PULL_SHA capture in Step 1; PROP-066's helper-script approach catches it via durable on-disk sentinel timestamps. Confirmed: ff-merge succeeded, sync-workspace was invoked, sentinel written.

scenarios.name: local-only commit ahead of remote (eventual-consistency lag)
scenarios.setup: work-init makes a local commit but doesn't push; sentinel ts=2026-05-31T08:40Z exists, origin HEAD ts older than sentinel
scenarios.interpretation: Benign case — LOCAL is ahead, no force-push, no sync needed. Audit-only sentinel written with divergent_files list.

scenarios.name: force-push: origin rewritten with divergent history
scenarios.setup: other-clone hard-resets, makes divergent commit, force-pushes to origin; work-init's HEAD is no longer an ancestor of origin and vice versa
scenarios.interpretation: Safety abort path fires correctly. Sentinel written under sync-workspace-non-ff-abort-*.json (NOT sync-workspace-runs-*.json) so the per-cycle audit count is preserved. Exit 1 signals to caller that something went wrong; caller's bash block does not propagate the exit code (best-effort).

additional_notes.config_file_read: Verified: helper reads monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.config.json on each invocation and uses fallback_baseline_at when no sentinels exist on disk.
additional_notes.always_writes_sentinel: Verified across all 4 scenarios: exactly one final sentinel file written per invocation (success, divergence, abort, or no-op). No silent early-returns. This is the diagnostic improvement over PROP-064.
additional_notes.cross_bash_session_safe: Verified: the helper takes no environment-variable inputs from previous bash tool calls. It derives every signal from durable on-disk state (config, sentinel directory, git CLI). Closes failure_mode_1 from PROP-066's diagnosis.
additional_notes.skip_by_omission_safe: Structural: the prompt-side invocation is 3 lines instead of 140 lines. Cannot be directly tested in synthetic — that's a property of the prompt, not the helper. Will be confirmed by post-deployment canary (sentinel count >= 18 in 24h).

next_step: Deploy commit + push + manual workspace-sync.md sync to FUSE per DIRECTIVE Step 7.
